projects.title: Arkaiv — AI-Powered Personal Operating System
projects.subtitle: React, Gemini, Sheets, OpenClaw
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.toolbox: React 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS 4, Express.js, Google Gemini AI, Google Sheets API, OpenClaw AI Agents (Scrum)
projects.techniques: AI multi-model routing, Local-first data architecture, PKM, AI-orchestrated Scrum, PWA
projects.results: A complete personal OS with 8 integrated views backed by Gemini AI
projects.content:
## Arkaiv — Ngarsip Harian, Unpacked

> "How can I build a single system that actually understands my daily life — and helps me run it better?"

In 2026, I built **Arkaiv** (Indonesian for "archive"), short for *Ngarsip Harian* — Daily Archiving. It started as a notes dump and evolved into a full personal operating system.

The app has **8 integrated views**: Money, Notes, Tasks, Shopping, Calendar, Focus, Library, and Summary — all powered by Google Gemini AI, local-first with Google Sheets sync.

**Core Insight**
- A single unified interface replaces the mental overhead of juggling 5+ separate tools
- AI works best when it observes passively and surfaces insights
- Google Sheets as a sync layer is surprisingly powerful: human-readable, versioned, free
- Multi-model AI routing (Gemini Flash for speed, Pro for depth) keeps the app fast and cheap
- Orchestrating AI agents with Scrum inside OpenClaw produced production-grade code at solo-founder speed

### Insight #1: Local-First, Sheets-Synced Architecture
Instead of a traditional database, Arkaiv uses local-first + Google Sheets sync. All data lives in the browser with Google Sheets as the persistent backend.
- **Zero infra costs:** No database servers
- **Human-readable backups:** Every entry is a row in Sheets
- **Batch parsing pipeline:** Custom batchParserCoordinator with canonicalization and validation
- **Spreadsheet reconciliation:** spreadsheetReconciler diffs local state against Sheets

### Insight #2: Multi-Model AI Routing
Not every AI task needs Gemini Pro — sometimes Flash is 10x cheaper. A custom parser model router classifies incoming text by complexity:
- Simple categorization → Flash
- Complex parsing → Pro
- Weekly summaries → Pro with structured prompts
- Chat queries → Context-aware routing

### Insight #3: Behavior Caching and Deep Work Mode
**Behavior Cache:** Learns recurring patterns — if you always categorize "kopi kenangan" as coffee, it stops asking. Cut parsing latency by 40%.
**Deep Work Transformer:** A Focus view that transforms tasks into timed deep-work blocks, synced with Calendar. Backed by spec NDC-008 with automated ship-gate validation.

### Insight #4: Building with AI Agents — OpenClaw Scrum
I orchestrated AI agents inside OpenClaw with Scrum: Product Owner (me), Engineering agents, QA agents, Design agents. The docs/ directory has 20+ spec documents, 15+ screenshot validation runs, automated benchmarks.

### Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React 19 + Vite 6 + Tailwind CSS 4, Framer Motion, Recharts
- **Backend:** Express.js 5, TypeScript, Vercel serverless
- **AI:** Google Gemini (Flash + Pro) with custom model router
- **Data:** Local-first + Google Sheets sync via API
- **Integrations:** Google Calendar API, Google OAuth
- **PWA:** Full offline via vite-plugin-pwa

## Strategic Takeaways
Arkaiv proved that a personal tool built with intention and modern AI can outperform polished SaaS. It demonstrated AI-orchestrated solo development producing production-grade, tested, documented software at team speed.

**Status:** Live & actively maintained. 
**Link:** notes-dump-zeta.vercel.app

projects.title: Olist Ecommerce Satisfaction Visualization
projects.subtitle: Power BI, Visualization
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.content:
## Olist Case Study, Unpacked

> "What truly separates a satisfied customer from a deeply disappointed one?"

**Core Insight**
1. A healthy average can hide operational illness
2. Products and sellers aren't the real culprits
3. Delivery delays trigger customer dissatisfaction
4. The shipping phase is the true bottleneck
5. Delay hotspots are route-specific
6. High cost ≠ fast delivery — financial inefficiencies exposed

### Insight #1: Surface-Level Health Can Hide Internal Dysfunction
![Olist Dashboard](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68727945c3f561671347630c_1.png)

With a solid 4.09 out of 5, leadership could've easily concluded everything was fine. But averages can lie — they mask extremes that cancel each other out.

### Insight #2: It's Not the Products or the Sellers
![Product Analysis](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872794533838b79611b3f0d_2.png)

Performance lines were largely flat — top categories and sellers showed no red flags. The obvious culprits were innocent.

### Insight #3: The Turning Point — Delay Drives Dissatisfaction
![On-Time vs Late](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68727945750d80a157082fbf_3.png)

On-time deliveries earned 4s and 5s. Late ones got 1s and 2s. Logistics was the #1 driver of customer experience.

### Insight #4: Diagnosing the Delay — Where's the Time Lost?
![Logistics Report](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872794581428b13e9127d9a_4.png)

Shipping time ballooned from 8 to 27+ days for late orders. The bottleneck was transit — the logistics partners.

### Insight #5: Mapping the Epicenter
![Delay Matrix](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687279445fe95b0ea228de3e_5.png)

Only certain routes were severely affected — especially SP to northeastern states.

### Insight #6: High Costs, Slow Deliveries — The Worst Combo
![Cost vs Time](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687279457d0489bf504f8247_7.png)

Olist was overpaying for subpar service — a double hit: poor experience and wasted budget.

## Strategic Takeaways
- Audit and renegotiate logistics partners on high-cost, high-delay routes
- Enforce stricter SLAs
- Use data as leverage

In modern e-commerce, delivery isn't just a service — it's part of the product.

projects.title: Essential Oils Customer Satisfaction Analysis
projects.subtitle: Python, Machine Learning
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.techniques: Sentiment analysis, topic modeling, explainable AI, regression, Kano classification
projects.industries: Consumer wellness, essential oils, digital marketplaces
projects.results: Targeted product insights, data-driven quality improvements, novel review-to-roadmap pipeline.
projects.content:
## Case Study: Decoding Customer Satisfaction for Essential Oils

> "Can we extract strategic product insights purely from online reviews?"

I explored 23,000+ reviews of essential oil products from Tokopedia to identify what truly drives satisfaction.

**Core Insight**
- Aroma and packaging are key drivers of 5-star reviews
- Customers are highly price-sensitive, especially mid-tier buyers
- Diffuser support is a "must" for premium consumers
- The Kano Model + SHAP approach reveals segment-specific strategies

### Insight #1: What People Really Talk About
![BERTopic](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872210461b8e8a2b3c69f32_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20154556.png)

Scraped 23,068 reviews using a custom Python pipeline. BERTopic extracted 11 Customer Satisfaction Dimensions — aroma, price, packaging, delivery, service.

### Insight #2: What Drives Ratings
![Sentiment](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68721ee0b304a9ed752578c3_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20153724.png)

GPT-4o-mini labeled reviews by dimension. Aroma had the strongest influence on 5-star reviews.

### Insight #3: Which Model Knows Best?
![MAE](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68722109e8c17297c0014e28_MAE%20Model.png)

XGBoost stood out with MAE of 0.125 and balanced performance across segments.

### Insight #4-5: SHAP + Kano Model
- **Performance:** Aroma, Packaging, Quality
- **Must-be:** Price (Mid), Diffuser Support (Premium)
- **Excitement:** Bonus items, Efficacy

### Segment Insights
![Low SHAP](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872226bb6ac4d56581cbd77_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20155216.png)
![Mid SHAP](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872226be740983f6e01f40d_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20155228.png)
![Premium SHAP](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872226b8df2ae8cbeaf1928_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20155238.png)

Customer feedback should be seen as a vital compass for product innovation, not noisy data.

projects.title: Telco Customer Churn Prediction
projects.subtitle: Python, EDA, Machine Learning
projects.author: Aruci Team (Bilal, Akhdan, Dendy)
projects.results: Lower churn, smarter targeting, service strategies rooted in behavioral data.
projects.content:
## Customer Churn, Unpacked

> "How can a telecom company turn churn into insight?"

**Core Insight**
- Long-tenured customers are more loyal
- Call center and digital services enhance retention
- High-end device users are a churn risk
- Credit-based payment users churn more
- New customers need nurturing within the first 5 months

7,000+ customer records from Jakarta and Bandung.

### Insight #1: Most Churn Happens Early
![Churn Early](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227ebb6ac4d56581e585d_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161413.png)
New customers in first 5 months at highest risk.

### Insight #2: Loyalty Lives in Usage
![Usage](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227eb93dde2a217c84db9_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161445.png)
Active service users significantly more likely to stay.

### Insight #3: High-End Device = High Risk
![Device](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227eb630a6b555231f3d8_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161504.png)
Premium device owners churn more — higher expectations, lower switching friction.

### Insight #4: Payment Style Matters
![Payment](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227ebf48c8590a9a9ee7a_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161534.png)
Credit and pulsa users churn at higher rates.

### Insight #5: Call Center Saves Customers
![Call Center](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227eb290c7dad4c1b09a1_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161552.png)
Call center interaction correlates with lower churn.

### Model
![Model](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687228302a3f1bf28668a00d_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161729.png)
XGBoost achieved balanced performance. If I could redo it, I'd make the model the central decision-support engine.

projects.title: Health Commodities Supply Chain by USAID Visualization
projects.subtitle: Tableau, Visualization
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.techniques: Operational dashboarding, multi-year KPI trend analysis
projects.results: Disrupted assumptions, data-backed narratives, strategic lessons.
projects.content:
## USAID Supply Chain Analysis, Unpacked

> "What's really happening inside the health commodities supply chain run by USAID?"

My first-ever visualization project using a proper BI tool. Dataset from Kaggle — simulated USAID health commodities supply chain.

**Core Insight**
- Sudden delivery volume collapse in 2015
- Air charter was eating the budget disproportionately
- RDC share shrinking year over year
- Nigeria dominated distribution
- Reliability and cost efficiency feed each other

### Insight #1-4: Key Findings
The 2015 delivery cliff, air charter costs, RDC decline, and Nigeria's dominance all painted a picture of a supply chain that needed strategic attention.

### Insight #5: The Reliability-Cost Gap
I realized I was tracking volumes and costs but not answering what matters: reliability (consistency, on-time rate, accuracy) and cost efficiency (shipment count, weight, freight cost). This gap analysis became the foundation for the dashboard redesign.

## Strategic Takeaways
A first draft dashboard is never the final answer. The real value comes from asking: what question did I not answer? Then going back and answering it.

projects.title: Maintenance Schedule and History Visualization
projects.subtitle: Looker, Visualization
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.toolbox: Looker Studio, Script App, Google Spreadsheets
projects.techniques: Operational dashboarding, multi-year KPI trend analysis
projects.results: Visualization of maintenance schedule and report
projects.content:
## First Hands-on Looker Studio

Practice project mocking up a Maintenance dashboard with dummy data based on real machine failure history.

### Dashboard Emphasis
2 pages: quick overview and detailed report.

#### Page #1: Maintenance Schedule Quick Overview
Color-coded grids for maintenance status. Google App Scripts integration enables direct spreadsheet manipulation — a fun experiment adding interactivity to an otherwise static dashboard.

#### Page #2: Maintenance Detailed Report
Bar charts of downtime per machine, failure type breakdown, workload distribution by PIC. Tables and location-based charts for tracking issues and spatial patterns.

projects.title: Mental Health Prediction App
projects.subtitle: Python, Machine Learning
projects.author: Yukai Mojikai Team (Izzan, Akhdan, Bagus, Rafi, Rifqi)
projects.results: Journaling App with Mental Health Analysis
projects.content:
## Mental State Classification Using BERT

I led development of a machine learning pipeline that classifies user text into five mental sentiment categories using a pre-trained BERT model and TensorFlow. Deployed as a scalable API on Google Cloud Run.

## Data Preprocessing
- Removed punctuation, numbers, whitespace, special characters, stop words
- BERT tokenizer with padding to 53 tokens
- Attention masks for valid tokens vs padding

## Building the Model
Pre-trained BERT from HuggingFace + custom TensorFlow layers: input layers, BERT embeddings, global max pooling, dense layers with ReLU + dropout, sigmoid output. Optimized with Adam. Score: 88.4.

## Deployment
Google Cloud Run for auto-scaling serverless hosting. REST endpoint accepting raw text, returning predicted sentiment classes.

## Final Thoughts
End-to-end NLP pipeline — from preprocessing to scalable deployment. Built not just a model but a complete sentiment classification service for real-world use.
